data.title: Perella Weinberg Partners 1Q 2026: Revenue $148.92M, EPS $0.02— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Perella Weinberg Partners reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenues down year-over-year and sharply lower net income and earnings per share as deal activity and fee-paying clients declined.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$148.92M$211.83M(29.7%)
Net income²$1.49M$17.34M(91.4%)
Diluted EPS³$0.02$0.24(91.7%)

¹ Reported as “Revenues”. ² Reported as “Net income (loss) attributable to Perella Weinberg Partners”. ³ Reported as “Net income (loss) per share attributable to Class A common shareholders”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenues fell 30% YoY to $148.9M, driven by fewer fee-paying clients and reduced transaction closings.
  • Client Mix & Fees: Total advisory clients declined to 62 from 74, and clients paying ≥$1M in fees fell to 28 from 39, although average fee per client increased.
  • Cost & Staffing Actions: Compensation expense declined 18% as discretionary bonuses fell; non-bonus compensation and equity awards rose due to new hires.
  • Operating Cash Flow: The quarter recorded operating cash outflows driven by first-quarter incentive payouts and lower receivables, putting near-term liquidity under pressure.
  • Strategy: The firm continues to invest in its platform and add senior hires to scale advisory capabilities despite volatile deal activity.

Original SEC Filing: Perella Weinberg Partners [ PWP ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: OCEANFIRST FINANCIAL CORP 1Q 2026: Revenue ($28K), Net income $20.51M, EPS $0.36— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
OCEANFIRST FINANCIAL CORP reported first-quarter 2026 results showing a small loss on loan sales, stable net income of $20.51M and diluted EPS of $0.36, compared with the prior-year quarter, according to its 10-Q filing.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹($28K)$858K(103.3%)
Net income²$20.51M$20.51M0%
Diluted EPS³$0.36$0.352.9%

¹ Reported as “Net (loss) gain on sales of loans”. ² Reported as “Net income available to common stockholders”. ³ Reported as “Diluted earnings per share”.

Business Highlights

  • Net interest income improved and net interest margin expanded 6 basis points to 2.93%, driven by higher loan and securities volumes and lower funding costs.
  • Loans grew roughly 3% on an annualized basis, led by commercial and industrial lending which rose about 19% annualized; continued emphasis on commercial real estate investor loans.
  • Deposits shifted toward core funding with time deposit runoff and reduced FHLB usage, lowering funding costs.
  • Non-interest expense fell 13% quarter-over-quarter despite merger and restructuring charges; the company discontinued residential origination.
  • Liquidity remained strong with approximately $3.9B of on-balance liquidity capacity and ongoing stress testing and capital planning supporting operations.

Original SEC Filing: OCEANFIRST FINANCIAL CORP [ OCFC ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Dime Community Bancshares 1Q 2026: Revenue $5.73M, Net income $32.76M, EPS $0.75— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Dime Community Bancshares reported first-quarter 2026 results with higher revenue and substantially improved profitability versus the year-ago quarter, driven by stronger net interest income and core operating performance.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$5.73M$4.64M23.4%
Net income²$32.76M$19.64M66.8%
Diluted EPS³$0.75$0.4566.7%

¹ Reported as “Service charges and other fees”. ² Reported as “Net income available to common stockholders”. ³ Reported as “Earnings per common share”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue momentum: Net interest income rose materially, supporting stronger core operating income and improved quarterly profitability.
  • Deposit & funding shift: Customer deposits declined notably, prompting closer liquidity monitoring and increased use of short-term investments.
  • Credit & asset quality focus: Elevated non-performing loans and higher provisions led to intensified credit reviews, stress testing and workout activity.
  • Lending activity: Business and real estate originations accelerated, with emphasis on non-owner occupied commercial real estate and multifamily portfolios.
  • Risk management: Strengthened ALCO oversight, enhanced loan review and stress testing, and regular board reporting to manage concentrated CRE exposure.

Original SEC Filing: Dime Community Bancshares, Inc. /NY/ [ DCOM ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: BYLINE BANCORP, INC. 1Q 2026: Revenue $154.2M, EPS $0.83— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
BYLINE BANCORP, INC. reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $154.2M and net income of $37.6M, driven by higher net interest income and lower credit provision versus the year-ago quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $154.2M for 1Q 2026, compared with $149.7M in 1Q 2025; YoY change 3.0%.
  • Net income was $37.6M for 1Q 2026, compared with $28.2M in 1Q 2025; YoY change 33.1%.
  • Diluted EPS was $0.83 for 1Q 2026, compared with $0.64 in 1Q 2025; YoY change 29.7%.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was supported by an $11.6M increase in net interest income and a reduction in the provision for credit losses.
  • SBA lending remained a growth engine; the bank was the most active 7(a) lender in Illinois for the quarter with strong loan servicing and sales activity.
  • Core deposit growth, including higher interest-bearing checking and time deposits, funded lending activity and improved funding mix.
  • Efficiency ratio improved to 49.78% due to higher net interest income and controlled non-interest expenses.
  • Asset quality was stable with a modest decline in non-performing assets and a calibrated reduction in credit loss provision.

Original SEC Filing: BYLINE BANCORP, INC. [ BY ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: NOVAG Announces Virtual Annual Meeting May 14, 2026; Focus on Donlin Gold Catalysts
data.description:
NOVAG announced its 2026 virtual Annual General Meeting to be held May 14, 2026 at 1:00 p.m. PDT and outlined post‑meeting strategic commentary.

Key Highlights:

  • Meeting set for May 14, 2026 at 1:00 p.m. PDT as a virtual‑only event to enhance accessibility and reduce carbon footprint.
  • Shareholders may vote by proxy, join the virtual webcast to vote, and submit questions during the meeting or in advance by email.
  • Agenda includes election of directors, auditor appointment, setting auditor remuneration, equity plan resolutions, and advisory votes on executive pay.
  • Post‑meeting remarks from Chairman Thomas S. Kaplan and CEO Greg Lang on gold strategy and latest catalysts for the Donlin Gold project.

Original SEC Filing: NOVAGOLD RESOURCES INC [ NG ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: MasterCraft to supplement proxy after stockholder complaints; DCF equity ranges disclosed
data.description:
MasterCraft is supplementing its joint proxy/prospectus after demand letters and lawsuits over alleged disclosure issues and is providing updated financial disclosure details.

Key Highlights:

  • MasterCraft will voluntarily supplement the Joint Proxy Statement/Prospectus to moot demand letters and two New York state court complaints without admitting liability.
  • Complaints allege negligent misrepresentation and seek to enjoin or rescind the mergers and recover fees; MasterCraft denies the allegations.
  • Wells Fargo DCF for MasterCraft implies equity value per share of $35.16–$38.80 (net cash ~ $81.4M) vs. $23.12 close on Feb 4, 2026.
  • Wells Fargo DCF for Marine Products gives implied ranges of $9.50–$10.54 and $13.00–$14.59 per share (net cash ~ $54.2M) vs. $9.94 close on Feb 4, 2026.

Original SEC Filing: MasterCraft Boat Holdings, Inc. [ MCFT ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: DeFi Development Launches $200 Million At-The-Market Stock Program With R.F. Lafferty
data.description:
DeFi Development entered into a Sales Agreement with R.F. Lafferty & Co. to offer and sell up to $200 million of its common stock through at-the-market offerings. The company may sell shares from time to time at its discretion, and either party can suspend or terminate the program, including on 10 days' notice. R.F. Lafferty will earn a cash commission of up to 0.75% of gross proceeds and be reimbursed for certain expenses. Net proceeds are intended for working capital, acquiring Solana (SOL) digital assets, and strategic initiatives.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: At-the-market offering sales agreement for common stock
  • Counterparty: R.F. Lafferty & Co.
  • Signed / Effective: May 01 2026 / May 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Raise capital for working capital, SOL purchases, and strategic initiatives

Original SEC Filing: DeFi Development Corp. [ DFDV ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: This AI-Enabled Manufacturer's Business Is Booming. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Proto Labs, a company that prototypes and manufactures difficult to make parts, reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings with the help of artificial intelligence and a new strategy.

The advanced manufacturing technology company on Friday reported first-quarter earnings per share of 54 cents on sales of $139.4 million, up 10.4% year over year. Wall Street was looking for about 40 cents in EPS and sales of $135 million, according to FactSet.

For the second quarter, Proto Labs projects 54 cents in EPS from sales of $144 million. Wall Street currently projects 47 cents in EPS and $144 million in sales.

It looks like a solid quarter. The stock gyrated wildly in response. Proto Labs stock was up almost 6% in premarket trading, above $68, but the gains faded. Shares traded as low as $57.67 before closing at $65.17, up 0.6%. The S&P 500 rose 0.3% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.3%.

Starting points might help explain the volatility. Coming into Friday's session, Proto Labs stock was up 28% this year and up 81% over the past 12 months. Those gains left shares trading for about 35 times earnings expected over the coming 12 months, up from 25 times a year ago.

Proto Labs excels at rapid prototyping and building custom parts for its customers using automated CNC machines, injection molds, and 3D printers. It is moving beyond prototyping with the help of AI, which can connect machines, assist with design, and make ordering easier for customers.

Proto Labs does a significant amount of business with medical technology, aerospace, and transportation firms.

"These results reflect strong demand for Protolabs' digital manufacturing services, and disciplined execution across the business," said CEO Suresh Krishna. "We're making continued progress across our strategic pillars while strengthening the organization to support long--term growth and scalability."

The company's so-called pillars are: enhancing the customer experience, serving them from prototyping to production; accelerating innovation with the help of AI; growing production; and improving efficiency.

"Each of our four strategic pillars reinforces the others, and collectively, they drive a more customer-centric, innovative, efficient, and scalable Protolabs," said Krishna in February.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: SPAC Blueport Acquisition, SingAuto Agree to $1.2B Merger
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Blueport Acquisition, a special purpose acquisition company, entered into a business combination agreement with SingAuto, a Singapore-based firm that provides technology solutions for electric vehicles, in a $1.2 billion deal.

The companies agreed to form a new holding company, which will be listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market. The company is expected to be led by SingAuto Chief Executive Yuqiang Lu.

SingAuto shareholders will receive about 120 million shares of the combined public company, valued at $10 each a share.

"We believe SingAuto is a uniquely compelling company with green cold-chain logistics technology solutions for smart commercial electric vehicles that will benefit from being a public company," Blueport Chief Executive William Rosenstadt said.

The deal has been approved by the boards of both companies and is expected to close by the end of the year.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: TSX erases weekly gain as energy and metal mining shares fall
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSX ends down 0.2% at 33,891.18
  • Energy declines 1.1% as oil settles nearly 3% lower
  • Materials group loses 1.1% as gold dips
  • Badger Infrastructure jumps 19.3% on Q1 revenue beat

By Tharuniyaa Lakshmi and Fergal Smith

Canada's main stock index dipped on Friday, leaving it barely changed for the week, as losses for resource shares offset gains for technology, and investors weighed prospects of diplomatic progress in the Iran war.

The Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX Composite Index ended down 73.15 points, or 0.2%, at 33,891.18. For the week, the index inched 0.04% lower, while it has advanced 6.9% since the start of the year.

Tehran has submitted its latest proposal for negotiations with the United States, Iranian state media and a Pakistani official said, a move that could offer hope for breaking a deadlock in efforts to end the Iran war.

"There’s going to be a lot of uncertainty until the tensions kind of die down," said Webull Canada CEO Michael Constantino.

The conflict has spurred inventory building in Canada's manufacturing sector, leading to the sector expanding in April at its fastest pace in nearly four years.

Energy fell 1.1% as oil gave back some recent gains. U.S. crude oil futures settled nearly 3% lower at $101.94 a barrel.

Imperial Oil missed analysts' estimates for first-quarter profit, as weaker crude realizations and unplanned outages at its facilities reduced refinery throughput. The oil producers' shares fell 4%.

The materials group , which includes metal mining shares, lost 1.1% as the price of gold edged lower.

Three of the 10 major sectors ended higher, including technology . It rose 1.9%, with shares of e-commerce company Shopify Inc up 5.2%.

Badger Infrastructure Solutions Ltd was a standout. Its shares jumped 19.3% after the excavating firm reported first-quarter revenue that beat estimates.

data.title: Innovative Industrial Properties Inc expected to post earnings of $1.07 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Innovative Industrial Properties Inc , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 4 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Park City Utah-based company is expected to report a 7.5% decrease in revenue to $66.341 million from $71.72 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Innovative Industrial Properties Inc is for earnings of $1.07 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 1.3% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Innovative Industrial Properties Inc is $50.00, about 7.8% below its last closing price of $54.25

data.title: DHI Group Inc expected to post earnings of 3 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• DHI Group Inc , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Centennial Colorado-based company is expected to report a 10.0% decrease in revenue to $29.065 million from $32.3 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 3 analysts, based on LSEG data.The company's guidance on February 4 2026, for the period ended March 31, was for revenue between $28.00 million and $30.00 million.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for DHI Group Inc is for earnings of 3 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for DHI Group Inc is $3.88, about 62.1% above its last closing price of $2.39

data.title: Veradermics Raises $30 Million in Private Pre-Funded Warrant Deal With Suvretta Affiliates
data.description:
Veradermics entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement for a concurrent private placement with entities affiliated with Suvretta Capital. The company sold pre-funded warrants to purchase 300,000 shares at $99.99999 per warrant, with a $0.00001 exercise price, generating approximately $30 million in gross proceeds. The deal closed alongside Veradermics' public offering of 3,843,790 shares at $100.00 per share, with a 30-day option for 576,568 additional shares. The warrants feature a 9.99% beneficial ownership cap, adjustable up to 19.99% with notice.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement for private placement of pre-funded warrants
  • Counterparty: Suvretta Capital affiliates
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 29 2026 / May 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until fully exercised
  • Reason: Raise capital alongside public offering

Original SEC Filing: Veradermics, Inc [ MANE ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Nexstar Media Group declares $1.86 quarterly cash dividend payable May 29
data.description:
Nexstar Media Group declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.86 per share.

Key Highlights:

  • Board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.86 per share on common stock.
  • Dividend payable May 29, 2026 to shareholders of record on May 15, 2026.
  • Company intends to pay regular quarterly dividends but future payouts reviewed quarterly at Board's discretion.

Original SEC Filing: NEXSTAR MEDIA GROUP, INC. [ NXST ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026
